MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT SUPREME GLASS COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests for Admission, Requests for Production of Documents, set one,and for sanctions, against Cross-Complainant, Moroso Construction, Inc.

Continued to March 18, 2020 per agreement of parties; email dated 2/19/2020.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mohammad A. Fakhreddine, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF MOHAMMAD ALI FAKHREDDINE, T:310.698.0804, for cross-defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT STYLEX LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT, as follows:

Stylex, LLC's demurrer to Supreme Glass Company's first amended cross-complaint is sustained in its entirety with leave to amend. The first amended cross-complaint alleges Stylex is a California limited liability company doing business as Coast to Coast Manufacturing and substituted Stylex wherever Coast to Coast appeared in the cross-complaint. According to its article of organization, Stylex did not exist until July 30, 2018. (RJN, Exh. 1.) The allegations in the cross-complaint concern events that occurred in 2012 and 2013. (Cross-Complaint 8, 14, 19, 20, 21.) The cross-complaint does not properly allege facts to support successor liability. (See McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 753 [successor corporation can be liable where "the transaction amounts to a consolidation or merger of the two corporations" or "the purchasing corporation is merely a continuation of the selling corporation."]; Hughes v. Western MacArthur Co. (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 951, 956 [trial court did not err in finding complaint deficient for plaintiff's failure to allege "an essential factor in this case-that she intended to hold [defendant] liable for acts committed by its predecessor corporation"].) The Court grants Supreme Glass Company 20 days leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, ultimate facts showing that Stylex is a successor entity of Coast to Coast Manufacturing.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org
